title: Closures and lifetime

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Closures and lifetime; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Closures and lifetime
sources.claim: A closure retains access to lexical bindings after the creating scope returns, observing the binding's current value.

sources.claim: A closure retains access to lexical bindings after the creating scope returns, observing the binding's current value.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A closure retains access to lexical bindings after the creating scope returns, observing the binding's current value.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A counter factory returns a function that updates its private count binding across calls. Your goal is to implement and trace a closure-based abstraction with independent instances. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Control flow and functions; Module loading baseline; Lexical scope and temporal dead zone; Parameters, rest and return contracts. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A closure retains access to lexical bindings after the creating scope returns, observing the binding's current value. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Captured objects can prolong memory, loop binding choice matters, and stale state can arise in framework callbacks. This draft therefore rejects: A closure stores a frozen copy of every captured primitive at creation time.

includes: Use retained lexical state for factories and callbacks while avoiding accidental stale or shared state.
Implement and trace a closure-based abstraction with independent instances.
excludes: Captured objects can prolong memory, loop binding choice matters, and stale state can arise in framework callbacks.

outcomes: Implement and trace a closure-based abstraction with independent instances.

examples.title: Closures and lifetime: concrete case
examples.situation: A counter factory returns a function that updates its private count binding across calls.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A counter factory returns a function that updates its private count binding across calls.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Closures retain bindings, not automatic frozen snapshots; later updates to the binding can be observed.
examples.result: The result follows because Closures retain bindings, not automatic frozen snapshots; later updates to the binding can be observed.
